title: Kokoona filiformis
taxonomy: Kokoona filiformis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Kokoona, which is a small group of plants native to tropical Africa. The species is further classified as a member of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants known for their distinctive flowers and seeds. Kokoona filiformis is native to tropical regions of Africa, where it grows as a small shrub or vine. Its classification is closely related to other members of the Annonaceae family, which are known for their diverse range of species and habitats.

intro: Kokoona filiformis, also known as the "Wild Jasmine", is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Kokoona filiformis include flavonoids, saponins, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kokoona filiformis is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of infusions, salves, and ointments to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
